zh.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor於1170年出生。她和John Allen至少有1個兒子。她去世於1205年享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor于1170年出生。她和John Allen至少有1个儿子。她去世于1205年享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: Trevor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: habitational name from any of numerous places in Wales, in particular the one near Denbigh, so named from Welsh tre(f) ‘homestead, settlement’ + a mutated form of mawr ‘large’.
Irish (Dublin and Down): variant of   Trower  .

variants.name: Trower
meanings: English (mainly Sussex and Surrey): variant of   Thrower  .
Irish (Leitrim): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Treabhair ‘descendant of Treabhar’, a byname meaning ‘industrious, prudent’. The name has been Anglicized as Trevor .
Americanized form of German Trauer, a habitational name for someone from Trauen in Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Trover
meanings: Possibly Irish: from Trower, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Treabhair (see Trevor ).

variants.name: Traver
meanings: Catalan: habitational name from any of the minor places called Traver, mainly in La Garrotxa and L'Empordà districts.
English: variant of   Trevor  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor was born in 1170. She had at least 1 son with John Allen. She died in 1205, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor est née en 1170. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John Allen. Elle est décédée en 1205, à l’âge de 35 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Trevorは1170年に生まれた。彼女はJohn Allenとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1205年に35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor(은)는 1170년 태어났다. 그녀는 John Allen(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1205년 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Trevor nasceu em 1170. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John Allen. Ela faleceu em 1205, com 35 anos.
